title: 10 best herbal tinctures for post nasal drip

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with post nasal drip because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, this compound soothes and calms irritated nasal passages and sinuses, reducing congestion and excess mucus production. Additionally, the expectorant properties of licorice help loosen and clear out thick phlegm, allowing for easier drainage and relief from the pesky sensation of post nasal drip. As a result, herbal licorice tinctures can provide significant relief for those suffering from this common and uncomfortable condition.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Licorice root's antitussive properties help to reduce coughing and alleviate congestion, which can exacerbate postnasal drip.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin, help to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to postnasal drip.

Mucilage-forming properties: Licorice root contains mucilages that can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the nose and throat, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.

Flavonoids (specifically isoflavones): These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the nasal mucosa, reducing irritation and post nasal drip.

Coumarins (specifically scopoletin): This phenolic compound exhibits anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help reduce nasal congestion, kill off bacteria that cause infections, and alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are commonly used to make tinctures for post nasal drip due to their expectorant properties, helping to thin mucus and promote its discharge.
Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are another part used to make tinctures for post nasal drip, as they contain compounds that help soothe and calm the mucous membranes.

Barks: Glycyrrhiza glabra barks are also used to make tinctures for post nasal drip, likely due to their antimicrobial properties, which can help combat underlying infections contributing to the condition.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 1 part of vegetable glycerin for tincture base.
Combine 1 part of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 to 8 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 1 part of vegetable glycerin and store the tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with post nasal drip because it contains compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. The tincture's antiviral and antibacterial properties also work to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the post nasal drip. Additionally, purple coneflower has been shown to possess mucolytic properties, breaking down mucus and making it easier to expel, providing relief from the uncomfortable sensation of excess mucus in the back of the throat and sinuses.
remedy_properties: Echinacein: Echinacein is an active compound found in purple coneflower that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating post-nasal drip.

Cichoric acid: Cichoric acid is another key component of purple coneflower that exhibits antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which can help combat infections and inflammation in the sinuses and upper respiratory tract, thereby reducing mucus production and relieving post-nasal drip symptoms.

Polyphenols: The polyphenolic compounds present in purple coneflower tincture have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce excessive mucus production and soothe the mucous membranes, thus alleviating congestion and post-nasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.

Alkaloids: Specifically, alkaloids like echinacoside and glycosides have been shown to exhibit antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, supporting the body's natural defenses against infections that may contribute to post nasal drip.

Caffeic Acid Derivatives: These phenolic compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate post nasal drip by protecting the mucous membranes from oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nasal congestion and post nasal drip.
Flowers: The flowers contain compounds that have been traditionally used to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the nose, reducing post nasal drip.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated using scissors for cleanliness.
Weigh 1 part dried echinacea flowers and leaves by mass and combine with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the echinacea mixture in the vodka for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the finished echinacea tincture in a dark glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The tannins present in dandelion also help to constrict blood vessels, further alleviating congestion and excess mucus production. Additionally, dandelion's bitter compounds stimulate digestion and promote the removal of excess phlegm from the body, thereby providing relief from postnasal drip and promoting a clearer respiratory tract. As a result, herbal dandelion tinctures can be an effective natural remedy for soothing postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and post-nasal drip.

Expectorant properties: The volatile oils and mucilages present in dandelion tincture stimulate coughing and promote the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract, helping to clear out excess phlegm and reduce post-nasal drip.

Antimicrobial properties: Dandelion tincture's antimicrobial compounds, such as taraxasterol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause sinus infections and exacerbate post-nasal drip, helping to prevent further infection and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene saponin helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to post nasal drip.

Luteolin: As a flavonoid phenolic compound, luteolin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing post nasal drip.

Inulin: While not a traditional medicinal constituent, inulin, a type of fructan, can help to stimulate the mucous membranes and promote the clearance of mucus and debris from the nasal passages, reducing post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for tinctures due to their high content of mucilages that help soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the nose and throat.
Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures because they contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that help to thin mucus and promote drainage.
remedy_recipe: Gather taraxacum officinale roots and flowers in quantities of 1 part herb to 2 parts solvent.
Combine the gathered herb with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar covering the contents completely.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Transfer the clear liquid to smaller dropper bottles for storage and use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with post nasal drip because it contains antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the nose, throat, and sinuses. The tannins in elderberry work to reduce excess mucus production, while the bioflavonoids and anthocyanins help to strengthen the mucous membrane's natural barrier function, reducing inflammation and congestion. By doing so, elder tinctures provide relief from postnasal drip, allowing for easier breathing, improved sinus drainage, and a reduced risk of secondary infections.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elderberry's antiviral properties can help combat viral infections that cause postnasal drip, such as the common cold and flu, by reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in elder tincture have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating discomfort associated with postnasal drip.
Expectorant properties: Elder tincture's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel excess mucus that contributes to postnasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
Saponins: The saponins in Sambucus nigra have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to combat infections and reduce nasal discharge associated with post nasal drip.

Volatile oils (Terpenes): The terpenes in Sambucus nigra, including limonene and borneol, have expectorant properties, helping to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages, relieving post nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
Leaves: They are used for their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages.
Fruits: They are used due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nose and throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves from a pesticide-free environment in late summer.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a single layer using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 6 hours.
Combine the dried sambucus nigra material with 500ml of 60% glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily with a dropper.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its ability to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nose, throat, and lungs. The mucilage properties in marshmallow root work to thicken and lubricate mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion. This natural anti-inflammatory also calms irritated tissues, allowing for clearer breathing and a reduction in postnasal drip symptoms. As a result, marshmallow tinctures offer a gentle yet effective solution for relieving the discomfort associated with postnasal drip.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and shields the mucous membranes in the throat and nasal passages, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in marshmallow tincture have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating postnasal drip symptoms.

Expectorant properties: Marshmallow tincture contains compounds that stimulate the clearance of mucus from the respiratory tract, helping to thin and loosen excess mucus and promote its easy removal, which can help to reduce postnasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrate molecules help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with post nasal drip.

Irritinin (a type of mucilage): This thick, protective compound forms a physical barrier on the mucous membranes, trapping allergens and irritants and preventing them from entering the nasal passages, thus alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.

Gallic acid (a type of phenolic acid): As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, gallic acid helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, promoting healing and reducing the frequency and severity of post nasal drip episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are most commonly used to make tinctures for post nasal drip because they contain high concentrations of mucilages, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nose and throat.

Leaves: Althaea officinalis leaves are also used, although less commonly, due to their ability to aid in the reduction of inflammation and provide additional soothing properties to the nasal passages.

Stems: Althaea officinalis stems are occasionally used, as they contain similar mucilages to the roots and leaves, but in smaller amounts, which can still contribute to the overall efficacy of the tincture.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried althaea officinalis root and 500ml of 70% vodka as the solvent.
Combine the dried root with 500ml of 70% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Label and store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Amur corktree tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the nasal passages. The tincture's expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, reducing congestion and relieving the feeling of drainage down the back of the throat. Additionally, Amur corktree's antimicrobial properties may help to combat underlying infections that can contribute to post nasal drip. By addressing the root causes of post nasal drip, Amur corktree tinctures provide effective relief from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Antitussive and expectorant properties: The saponins present in Amur Corktree tincture help to reduce coughing and relieve congestion, making it easier for the body to expel mucus from the nasal passages.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Amur Corktree tincture have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to post-nasal drip symptoms by reducing swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses.

Mucilaginous properties: The mucilage present in Amur Corktree tincture forms a protective barrier that helps soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing the production of excess mucus and relieving congestion.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby reducing post nasal drip.

Phellodendrine: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections that may be contributing to post nasal drip, and also has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce nasal congestion and discharge.
Catechin: Possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Barks: Barks are used because they contain berberine, a compound that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain phellodendrine, a compound that has expectorant properties, helping to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages.

Roots: Roots are used because they contain berberine and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce swelling and combat infections causing post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the dried phellodendron amurense bark in quantities of 250-500 grams from a trusted supplier or local market.
Combine the dried bark with 80% ethanol or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every 2-3 days to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and label them with the date and contents for future reference.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. The tannins present in comfrey also have astringent properties that can help to dry up excess mucus, providing relief from the constant sensation of dripping or running nose. Additionally, comfrey's soothing and calming effects on the mucous membranes can help to ease discomfort and promote healing of the nasal tissues, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Comfrey's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.

Antihistaminic property: Comfrey contains histamine-reducing compounds that can help calm histamine-related symptoms like runny nose, sneezing, and postnasal drip, providing relief from allergic reactions and sinusitis.

Mucolytic property: Comfrey's mucolytic agents, such as allantoin, help break down excess mucus in the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and facilitating easier clearance of the nasal passages, which can help alleviate postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Allantoin is a glycosidic compound that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with post nasal drip.
Rutoside: Rutoside, a flavonoid glycoside, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.

Symphytine: Symphytine, a sesquiterpene lactone, exhibits anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties, helping to break down and clear excess mucus in the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby relieving post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part, as they contain a high concentration of mucilages, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes.
Roots: The roots are also used due to their high content of mucilages and other compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the nasal passages.
Stems: The stems are another part used in tincture production, as they contain a mix of mucilages and other bioactive compounds that help to calm and protect the mucous membranes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves and roots in the morning to ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Clean and dry the harvested symphytum officinale leaves and roots to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Use a 1:2 ratio of plant material to solvent such as 250ml of 80% ethanol and add it to the dried plant material.
Steep the plant material in the solvent for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture and discard the solids then store it in a clean dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cherry tinctures helps with post nasal drip because they contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that target the underlying causes of this annoying condition. The anthocyanins in cherry tincture have been shown to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, while its natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, providing quick relief from congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, cherry tincture's antimicrobial properties may also help combat underlying infections that can contribute to postnasal drip.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Cherry tincture's antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate postnasal drip symptoms.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds in cherry tincture help loosen and thin mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion associated with postnasal drip.
Antiviral properties: Cherry tincture's antiviral compounds can help combat viral infections that can cause postnasal drip, such as the common cold or sinusitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating post-nasal drip symptoms.

Phenolic Acids (e.g., Chlorogenic Acid): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities that can help combat infections and inflammation in the nasal tract, thereby reducing post-nasal drip.

Glycosides (e.g., Amygdalin): These plant-derived compounds have been shown to possess expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, including the nasal passages, which can help alleviate post-nasal drip.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits are used due to their high content of anthocyanins and other antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and soothe the nasal passages.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their salicylic acid content, which helps to reduce inflammation and thin mucus, making it easier to expel.
Barks: The barks are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to dry up excess mucus and reduce nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest prunus avium flowers and leaves in early summer when they are in full bloom for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the prunus avium flowers and leaves thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Combine 1 part of prunus avium flowers and leaves with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its natural decongestant properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. The menthol content in peppermint also acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the throat and nasal passages. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, providing fast relief from postnasal drip symptoms such as congestion, coughing, and runny nose.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Herbal peppermint tinctures can help loosen and clear excess mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The menthol in peppermint oil has natural anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to postnasal drip.
Cooling and soothing properties: Peppermint tincture's cooling sensation can help calm and soothe irritated mucous membranes, reducing discomfort and coughing associated with postnasal drip.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with post nasal drip by acting as a natural decongestant and expectorant, reducing nasal congestion and promoting the clearance of mucus from the nasal passages.
Limonene: Limonene, a monoterpenoid, has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and kill bacteria that can contribute to post nasal drip.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nasal congestion and inflammation, making it easier to expel mucus and alleviate post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part for making tinctures to treat post nasal drip due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have decongestant and expectorant properties.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are also used to make tinctures for post nasal drip relief, as they contain similar compounds to the leaves, such as menthol and menthone.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, or underground stems, are another part of the plant used to make tinctures for post nasal drip, due to their ability to be distilled for their menthol and menthone content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and transfer it to a clean glass jar.
Combine the chopped material with 500ml of 30% ethanol in the jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated nasal passages and sinuses. The herbal extract contains thymol, a powerful essential oil that has been traditionally used to ease congestion and reduce mucus production. When taken as a tincture, thyme's natural decongestant properties help to thin out excess mucus, making it easier to expel, thereby providing relief from the discomfort of post nasal drip.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, help combat bacterial and viral infections in the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing the severity of postnasal drip symptoms.

Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant compounds, such as eucalyptol, help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and alleviating congestion associated with postnasal drip.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to postnasal drip symptoms like swelling and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol is a monoterpene that has antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause post nasal drip.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol is a phenolic monoterpene that has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a polyphenolic compound that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nasal passages, sinuses, and throat, alleviating post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oils, which contain thymol, a compound that helps reduce mucus and alleviate congestion.
Stems: They contain a high concentration of thymol and other volatile compounds that aid in relieving respiratory issues, including post-nasal drip.
Buds: They are rich in essential oils and have antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections that may be contributing to post-nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the morning when they are at their highest oil content.
Chop 250 grams of the fresh leaves and add them to a glass jar.
Combine 750 milliliters of 80% vodka with the chopped leaves and seal the jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, rosemary can help reduce congestion in the sinuses and throat, relieving the discomfort caused by excess mucus. The essential oils present in the tincture also have decongestant and expectorant qualities, which help to clear out the nasal passages and ease coughing. As a result, herbal rosemary tinctures can provide fast and effective relief from postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Rosemary's antimicrobial properties help to combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses that can contribute to postnasal drip, reducing inflammation and congestion.

Decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects: Rosemary's decongestant properties help to reduce nasal swelling and ease congestion, while its anti-inflammatory properties alleviate discomfort and pain associated with sinus pressure and drainage.
Expectorant properties: Rosemary's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear excess mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe and reducing postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with post nasal drip.
Leaves: Fresh rosemary leaves contain essential oils that help to loosen mucus and reduce coughing, providing relief from the discomfort of post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning for optimal potency and flavor.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the washed leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine one part chopped rosmarinus officinalis leaves with two parts vodka or grain alcohol in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four weeks shaking daily for optimal tincture strength.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The herb's flavonoids and terpenes work together to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion and discomfort. Additionally, pot marigold's natural antihistamine compounds help to block histamine receptors, reducing excess mucus production and subsequent drips down the back of the throat. By addressing both inflammation and excess mucus, pot marigold tinctures provide effective relief from post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects: Pot marigold tincture's flavonoids and glycosides have been shown to reduce histamine release, thereby alleviating nasal congestion, itching, and sneezing associated with postnasal drip.

Mucolytic properties: The saponins in pot marigold tincture can break down thick mucus, making it easier to expel, and helping to relieve the sensation of a persistent cough or tickle at the back of the throat.

Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold tincture's terpenes and sesquiterpenes have antibacterial and antifungal effects, which can help combat infections that contribute to postnasal drip, such as sinusitis and upper respiratory tract infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing congestion and discomfort associated with post nasal drip.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain mucilage, a natural gel-like substance that helps to soothe and calm irritated nasal passages, providing relief from post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers during peak blooming season for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 6 hours.
Combine one cup of dried calendula flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete coverage.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and discarding solids using cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles, storing it in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months or until consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce congestion and soothe a sore throat. The citral content in lemongrass has been shown to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and alleviating the sensation of excess phlegm in the back of the throat. Additionally, lemongrass's calming effect on the mucous membranes can help to reduce inflammation and irritation, providing fast relief from postnasal drip symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, alleviating postnasal drip symptoms.

Decongestant properties: The essential oils present in lemongrass, such as citral and limonene, have natural decongestant properties that can help open up airways, reducing mucus production and relieving stuffiness associated with postnasal drip.

Expectorant properties: Lemongrass contains saponins, which are natural expectorants that can help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the nasal passages, throat, and chest, providing relief from postnasal drip and related congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving congestion and reducing inflammation associated with post nasal drip.
Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes contain a high amount of citral, a natural decongestant that helps to clear nasal passages and ease sinus pressure caused by post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and chop them into small pieces weighing approximately one ounce by volume.
Combine the chopped herb with 80 proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar at a ratio of one part herb to four parts solvent.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks shaking it daily to extract the bioactive compounds from the plant material.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
Transfer the final tincture to small amber bottles storing them in the refrigerator at 40 degrees fahrenheit to preserve its potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have a decongestant effect that reduces excess mucus production and thin out the mucus already present, making it easier to expel. Additionally, ginger's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out congestion, providing quick relief from postnasal drip discomfort and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Ginger helps loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and relieving postnasal drip by thinning mucus and making it easier to expel.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to postnasal drip and alleviate its symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the body from free radical damage caused by oxidative stress, which can exacerbate postnasal drip and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is the thick, underground stem of the ginger plant, rich in bioactive compounds that help to alleviate congestion and reduce mucus production associated with postnasal drip.
Roots: Ginger roots are used to make tinctures that combat inflammation and ease sinus pressure, making them an effective treatment for post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger root and clean it thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned ginger root into small pieces and combine it with 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 4 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a cool dark place and use it within 2 years taking 20-30 drops three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tinctures helps with post nasal drip because they possess natural antihistamines and decongestants that soothe the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing excess mucus production. The bioactive compounds in these tinctures also calm inflamed tissues, easing congestion and discomfort. By targeting the root causes of postnasal drip, herbal tea tinctures provide a gentle yet effective solution to alleviate symptoms, promoting a clearer airway and improved breathing. This natural approach can bring welcome relief from chronic nasal drip and related sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Herbal teas such as thyme, peppermint, and eucalyptus have expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing congestion and postnasal drip.

Antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects: Tinctures made from herbs like slippery elm, marshmallow root, and ginger can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating postnasal drip and other symptoms associated with allergies or sinusitis.

Antiviral properties: Herbs like echinacea, goldenseal, and licorice root have antiviral properties that can help combat viral infections such as the common cold and flu, which often cause postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, the roots help to break down mucus and reduce congestion associated with post nasal drip.
Buds: The delicate buds are rich in flavonoids and antioxidants that soothe irritated mucous membranes and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried camellia sinensis leaves in the morning when they are most potent.
Chop 2 cups of the harvested leaves into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka or rum.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking daily to extract maximum benefits.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years taking 10-15 drops three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that reduces inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, helping to alleviate congestion and clear out mucus buildup. Additionally, St John's Wort has natural decongestant properties that help to shrink swollen blood vessels and reduce excess mucus production, providing relief from postnasal drip symptoms such as coughing, sneezing, and nasal discharge. By addressing the underlying causes of postnasal drip, St John's wort tinctures can provide effective relief for those suffering from this uncomfortable condition.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: St. John's Wort contains compounds like hyperforin, which can help combat viral and bacterial infections in the nasal passages, reducing postnasal drip.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort have been shown to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion and postnasal drip symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in St. John's Wort may help protect the mucous membranes in the nasal passages from oxidative stress and damage caused by environmental toxins, further reducing postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hypericin, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that helps to reduce congestion and soothe irritated nasal passages.
Flowers: Containing hyperforin, a potent antiviral compound that targets the underlying causes of postnasal drip and sinusitis.
Seeds: Known for their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear excess mucus from the nasal cavity and sinuses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and stems, carefully avoiding contamination.
Weigh out 250 grams of the freshly harvested herb material for a single batch.
Combine the herb material with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for at least four weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, filling clean amber bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and natural expectorants. The berries contain a high concentration of vitamin C, which helps to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. Additionally, highbush cranberry's natural antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to post nasal drip. By promoting healthy mucus production and reducing congestion, highbush cranberry tinctures provide relief from persistent drips and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Highbush cranberry tincture's antimicrobial properties help to combat infections in the nasal passages and sinuses, reducing inflammation and congestion associated with postnasal drip.

Astringent properties: The astringent properties of highbush cranberry tincture help to dry up excess mucus and reduce its viscosity, making it easier to expel and providing relief from postnasal drip symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Highbush cranberry tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to postnasal drip and provide relief from discomfort and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a tincture that helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from postnasal drip.
Barks: The barks contain compounds that help to thin mucus and reduce its viscosity, making it easier to expel and relieving symptoms of postnasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned berries with 500ml of vodka in a clean glass jar at 10% herb-to-liquid ratio.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4 weeks in a cool dark place to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in the refrigerator for up to 2 years or use it immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The eucalyptol in the tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and relieving discomfort. Additionally, the menthol present in the tincture helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel. By soothing and calming the mucous membranes, Tasmanian blue gum tinctures provide effective relief from post nasal drip, allowing for a clearer and more comfortable breathing experience.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains saponins and phenolic acids that help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby reducing postnasal drip.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins present in Tasmanian Blue Gum have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and excess mucus production that contributes to postnasal drip.

Antimicrobial properties: The essential oils present in Tasmanian Blue Gum, such as eucalyptol and globul, have antimicrobial activity against bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which can help to combat underlying infections or inflammation that may be contributing to postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, Tasmanian Blue Gum roots help to reduce swelling and congestion associated with post nasal drip.
Barks: The barks contain antiseptic properties that aid in fighting off infections that can cause post nasal drip.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped eucalyptus leaves with 40% vodka in a clean glass jar for soaking.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for 14 days to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after 24 hours.
Transfer the filtered liquid into dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with post nasal drip because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When taken as a tincture, chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids work to calm the mucous membranes in the nose and throat, reducing inflammation and congestion that can exacerbate post nasal drip. This natural remedy also has an expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear out excess mucus, providing quick relief from congestion and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Chamomile tincture's ability to reduce histamine release and inflammation in the body helps alleviate postnasal drip symptoms, such as congestion and irritation.
Sothing and Calming Effects: Chamomile's soothing and calming properties can help relax the nasal passages and throat tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with postnasal drip.

Antioxidant Properties: Chamomile tincture's antioxidant compounds help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, including postnasal drip.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and congestion associated with post nasal drip.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing post nasal drip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated and before midday.
Weigh out 200g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers or 400g of fresh flowers for a single batch.
Combine the herbal material with 700ml of 95% ethanol in a glass container with a lid for a ratio of 1:3.5 parts herb to solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the container daily for at least 30 seconds to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label with preparation date ingredients and dosage instructions.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for post nasal drip are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, and botanicals that help alleviate symptoms associated with excess mucus production in the nasal passages. These natural remedies work by soothing and thinning mucus, reducing congestion, and fighting off underlying infections that can cause post nasal drip. Examples of herbal tinctures that effectively combat post nasal drip include Thyme, Echinacea, and Goldenseal, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. By using these tinctures, people with post nasal drip can experience significant relief from discomfort and congestion, allowing them to breathe easier, sleep better, and live more comfortably.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to post nasal drip that are treated with herbal tinctures are sinusitis, allergies, and bronchial congestion. Tinctures made from herbs such as Goldenseal, Echinacea, and Ginkgo Biloba help to reduce inflammation and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from these conditions. Additionally, tinctures containing herbs like Peppermint and Thyme can soothe a sore throat and ease congestion, offering comprehensive support for respiratory health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with post nasal drip is a blend of Echinacea, Thyme, and Eucalyptus. Echinacea supports the immune system and reduces inflammation, while Thyme has antimicrobial properties that combat bacterial and fungal infections. Eucalyptus, rich in expectorants, helps to clear mucus and congestion from the nasal passages. Taking these tinctures individually or in combination, 2-3 times a day, can help alleviate symptoms and promote healing. Consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Alternative therapies in health and medicine, herbal tinctures derived from traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) have shown potential benefits in treating upper airway cough syndrome (UACS), also known as postnasal drip syndrome. The study, which analyzed 16 studies involving 1355 participants, found that patients treated with Chinese medicinal herbs (CMH) experienced a higher recovery rate, better relief of primary symptoms, including cough and postnasal dripping, and a reduction in physical signs associated with UACS. This suggests that herbal tinctures may be a safe and effective alternative for managing postnasal drip symptoms, although further high-quality randomized controlled trials are needed to confirm these findings.
